headline: Ukraine confirm at 50.0 functional. Emergency-trigger context: dueling Victory Day ceasefires (Russia May 8-9, Ukraine May 5-6) both collapsed by May 7; Ukraine reported 1,820 Russian violations within hours of midnight May 5 ceasefire start. Per methodology v1.2 bad-faith-ceasefire rule already applied May 6 cycle: violations register as inverted signal toward Russia (counterparty), not Ukraine. New evidence May 6-7: Russia continued strikes through Victory Day window (108 combat drones + 3 missiles overnight May 5-6 striking Kharkiv; 22 Ukrainian killed in pre-ceasefire strikes); Zelenskyy slammed Russia ceasefire as 'theatre.' No new Ukraine-side ACT/INT-erosive evidence in 24h window. Confirm sustained from May 6 cycle. Sub-threshold drift; functional band sustained. May 9-10 emergency re-queue maintained for post-Victory Day evaluation.
key_evidence: May 5 2026 midnight: Ukraine-declared unilateral ceasefire began; Zelenskyy reported 1,820 Russian violations by 10:00am May 6 (per May 6 cycle).
May 5-6 overnight: Russia launched 108 combat drones + 3 missiles striking Kharkiv and other major cities; 22 Ukrainian civilians killed in pre-Russian-ceasefire strikes.
May 6: Zelenskyy publicly rejected Russia's May 8-9 ceasefire as not credible following Russian violations of Ukraine's ceasefire.
Russia May 8-9 Victory Day parade scaled down — first time in nearly two decades without tanks, missiles, military equipment — reportedly due to Ukrainian drone deterrence.
Per methodology v1.2 bad-faith-ceasefire rule applied May 6: ceasefire violations by Russia register as inverted signal toward Russia (counterparty), not Ukraine.
No new Ukraine-side ACT/INT-erosive evidence in May 6-7 24h window.

evidence.source: Defense News — Dueling Victory Day ceasefires for war in Ukraine collapse almost immediately
evidence.finding: Both ceasefires collapsed by May 7; escalating air strikes; massive missile-strike rhetoric for Kyiv.

evidence.source: NPR — Zelenskyy slams Russia as strikes kill 22 in Ukraine before announced ceasefire
evidence.finding: 22 Ukrainian civilians killed in pre-ceasefire Russian strikes; Zelenskyy public rejection of Russia ceasefire framing.

evidence.source: Kyiv Independent — Ukraine rejects Russia's Victory Day truce after Moscow violates Kyiv's ceasefire
evidence.finding: Ukrainian official: 'We just don't see the point (to follow it) for the parade'; documented Russian violations of Ukrainian ceasefire.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 confirmed. A1 detection: Ukrainian armed forces and civil-defense reporting infrastructure functional through ceasefire collapse; A4 amplification active (Zelenskyy public communications, civil-defense reporting).
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.0 confirmed. E1 affective resonance: Ukrainian state response acknowledges civilian-harm patterns (pre-ceasefire strike fatalities, Kharkiv strikes).
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.5 confirmed. AC2 proportionality: ceasefire-declaration itself is a proportionate state-action; AC4 response: civil-defense and air-defense response sustained through Victory Day window.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.0 confirmed. B3 scope clarity: ceasefire-declaration framing maintains civilian-protection boundary; bad-faith-ceasefire rule preserves Ukraine's BND.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.0 confirmed. AB2 transparency: Zelenskyy public reporting of violation counts (1,820 by May 6 morning) maintains accountability framework.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 confirmed. I1 consistency: declared civilian-protection commitment maintained through ceasefire window; bad-faith-ceasefire rule applied — violations attributed to Russia as counterparty.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction confirms 50.0. Sub-threshold drift; functional band sustained. May 9-10 emergency re-queue maintained.

negativeSignals: Active war context; civilian casualties from pre-ceasefire Russian strikes (22 killed May 5-6); ceasefire-collapse pattern; ongoing displacement and infrastructure damage.
positiveSignals: Ukrainian ceasefire-declaration discipline; public-reporting transparency on violation counts; civil-defense and air-defense response capability; international-coalition support sustained; Russia Victory Day parade scaled down (Ukrainian drone deterrence inverted-signal positive); per methodology v1.2 bad-faith-ceasefire rule applied — Russia bears violation cost.
sector_context: Eastern Europe / active-conflict cluster. Cross-reference: Russia (counterparty), Belarus (counterparty-adjacent). Ukraine distinguished by ceasefire-declaration discipline, civil-defense infrastructure, and bad-faith-ceasefire rule application preserving functional-band placement.
open_actions_resolved: Bad-faith-ceasefire rule re-applied (Russia bears violation cost)
No new Ukraine-side ACT/INT-erosive evidence in 24h window
open_actions_carried: May 9-10 emergency re-queue: post-Victory Day evaluation of Russian behavior and any Ukrainian-side civilian-protection deviation
Watch: any massive missile strike on Kyiv as threatened in pre-Victory Day rhetoric
Watch: any Ukrainian-side action that would invert bad-faith-ceasefire rule application
